question.text: Since February 2018, SEBI has made it mandatory for mutual funds to use which type of benchmark?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Since February 2018, SEBI mandated that all mutual fund schemes must use the Total Return Index (TRI) as their benchmark instead of the Price Return Index. TRI gives a fairer comparison because it includes dividends received from index companies.
**The Trap:** Before 2018, funds used Price Return Index (PRI) which excluded dividends. This made fund performance look better in comparison. TRI leveled the playing field.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in Series V-A. The date (February 2018) and the mandate (TRI) are frequently tested together.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If an investor compares old and new performance data, remember that post-2018 data uses TRI benchmarks. This means the benchmark returns appear higher than before, making it a tougher comparison for fund managers.
